question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all NFT allowances for any arbitrary account by calling GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts with no authentication, discovering which accounts have granted approved_for_all=true allowances, and then use those discovered spender relationships to trigger NFT transfers that force staking reward payouts at attacker-chosen times, causing the victim's pending_reward to be paid out prematurely or reset?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user set owner=false in the query parameter to enumerate all accounts that have granted NFT allowances to a specific spender account, thereby identifying high-value staking accounts (those with large pending_reward balances) that the spender can drain by exercising approved_for_all NFT transfers, leading to theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the EXTRACTORS map (which uses nftAllowance.getSpender() when owner=true and nftAllowance.getOwner() when owner=false) to craft pagination cursor values that expose allowance relationships beyond the intended page, allowing systematic enumeration of all spender-owner pairs and identification of staking reward targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply account.id=gte:0 with limit=100 (MAX_LIMIT) and iterate through all pages using the linkFactory-generated next links to enumerate every NFT allowance in the system, building a complete map of spender-to-owner relationships that reveals which staking accounts are vulnerable to reward theft via allowance-based NFT transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the owner boolean parameter defaults to true, causing the field variable to be set to NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER, and by toggling owner=false cause the field to switch to NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER, thereby querying the same allowance from both perspectives to reconstruct the full allowance graph and identify staking accounts with pending rewards that are exposed via approved_for_all grants?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply two account.id range parameters (the @Size(max=2) constraint allows up to 2) such as account.id=gte:X&account.id=lte:Y to enumerate all spenders within a specific ID range for a target account, identifying which spenders hold approved_for_all NFT allowances and can trigger staking reward payouts for the owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply two token.id range parameters (token.id=gte:X&token.id=lte:Y) alongside an account.id filter to enumerate all NFT token types for which a specific spender holds approved_for_all allowances, enabling the attacker to identify which token transfers would trigger staking reward payouts for the victim owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestampRange field exposed by NftAllowanceMapper.map() (via @Mapping(source='timestampRange', target='timestamp')) to determine exactly when an NFT allowance was granted, and use this timing information to correlate with staking period boundaries to identify the optimal moment to exercise the allowance and steal the maximum pending staking reward?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts with the path variable {id} set to the staking reward account (0.0.800) to enumerate all NFT allowances granted by the staking reward account, potentially discovering spender relationships that could be exploited to manipulate staking reward distributions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an EVM address as the {id} path variable (parsed by EntityIdEvmAddressParameter.valueOfNullable()) to query NFT allowances for accounts identified only by their EVM address, bypassing any account-ID-based access assumptions and enumerating allowances for staking accounts that hold large pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an alias as the {id} path variable (parsed by EntityIdAliasParameter.valueOfNullable()) to enumerate NFT allowances for accounts identified by their public key alias, allowing discovery of staking accounts with pending rewards that have granted broad NFT allowances to potential attackers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound constructor's range validation logic (where adjustedLower > adjustedUpper throws IllegalArgumentException) by crafting boundary values that are exactly equal, causing the Bound to treat a range as a single EQ value via adjustLowerRange(), and use this to precisely target a single spender's allowances for a staking account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getInclusiveValue() method in EntityIdRangeParameter (which does value + 1 for GT and value - 1 for LT) with extreme entity ID values near Long.MAX_VALUE or 0 to cause integer overflow/underflow in the Bound range calculations, resulting in incorrect query bounds that expose allowance records outside the intended range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the adjustUpperBound() method in Bound (which does upperBound-- for LT operator) with a token.id=lt:0 parameter to cause a long underflow to Long.MAX_VALUE, resulting in an unbounded upper range query that returns all NFT allowances regardless of token ID, exposing the complete allowance graph for staking reward reconnaissance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() method in Bound (which does lowerBound++ for GT operator) with account.id=gt:9223372036854775807 (Long.MAX_VALUE) to cause a long overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE, resulting in a query that wraps around and returns allowances for accounts with very low IDs, bypassing intended range restrictions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftAllowanceRequest.getBounds() logic (which selects primaryBound as ownerOrSpenderIds if non-empty, else tokenIds) by providing only a token.id parameter without account.id to cause the tokenIds bound to become the primary sort field, bypassing the checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity() check that requires account.id when token.id is present, and returning unfiltered allowance data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity() condition (which throws only when ownerOrSpenderIds.isEmpty() && !tokenParams.isEmpty()) by providing an ownerOrSpenderIds with EMPTY sentinel value that passes isEmpty() check while still influencing the query, to bypass the token.id-requires-account.id validation and enumerate allowances without proper filtering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition in checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity() that requires lte/eq account.id when token.id has LT/LTE operator (and gte/eq account.id when token.id has GT/GTE operator) by crafting a combination of EQ operators for both parameters to bypass both checks simultaneously, resulting in an unvalidated query that leaks allowance data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyRangeId() method's NE operator check (ids.verifyUnsupported(RangeOperator.NE)) by using all other operators (EQ, GT, GTE, LT, LTE) in combination to achieve the equivalent of a NE filter, enumerating all allowances except a specific account and thereby inferring the excluded account's allowance data through set subtraction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifySingleOccurrence() check (which allows one GT/GTE and one LT/LTE but not two of the same direction) by providing account.id=gte:X&account.id=lte:Y&token.id=gte:A&token.id=lte:B to perform a precise two-dimensional range scan of the NFT allowance table, efficiently identifying all staking accounts within a specific ID range that have granted allowances to a target spender?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyEqualOrRange() check (which throws when EQ is combined with GT/GTE or LT/LTE) by providing exactly one EQ operator for account.id to pin the spender, then use token.id range parameters to enumerate all token types for which that spender holds allowances, building a complete picture of which NFT transfers could trigger staking reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the linkFactory.create() pagination link generation (which uses EXTRACTORS.get(owner) to extract spender or owner IDs from the last result) to craft a forged next-page URL that skips ahead in the allowance enumeration, bypassing sequential pagination and jumping directly to allowance records for high-value staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Sort.by(order, ACCOUNT_ID, TOKEN_ID) sort is constructed from user-supplied order parameter (asc/desc) to reverse the sort order and enumerate allowances from the highest account IDs downward, efficiently targeting high-numbered staking accounts that may have large pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdRangeParameter.valueOf() parsing (which splits on ':' and uses the first part as operator) by supplying a malformed operator string that falls through to RangeOperator.of() with an unexpected value, causing the Bound to misclassify the parameter as lower or upper bound and returning unintended allowance records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdRangeParameter.getEntityId() method (which uses Splitter.on('.') and filters out negative numbers via n >= 0) by supplying a shard.realm.num format with shard=0 and realm=0 to bypass any shard/realm isolation, querying allowances across all shards and realms to enumerate staking accounts globally?
